board.title: Compilers
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: In the 1940s, programmers wrote assembly by hand. FORTRAN arrived in 1957 as one of the first high-level compiled languages. Since then, compiler optimization techniques have advanced, turning high-level code into more efficient machine instructions.

clues.question: Led the FORTRAN compiler project, helping prove compilers could generate efficient assembly; co-invented Backus–Naur Form
Structural correctness of code, usually checked before deeper semantic analysis
Limited CPU storage that compilers allocate values to before spilling to memory
Build tool that combines object files into an executable by resolving symbols statically or dynamically
Compiler stage that converts source code into typed tokens for easier parsing, often built with state machines
Compiler optimization that replaces constant expressions with computed values at compile time rather than runtime
IBM researcher who pioneered compiler optimization such as instruction scheduling and automatic parallelization; first woman to win the Turing Award
Class of low-level languages that map closely to machine instructions, such as x86 or ARM
Formal description of a language that parsers rely on to validate token sequences
Created the A-0 system in 1951, often considered the first compiler, and pioneered compiler technology
Consumes tokens and builds a parse tree or abstract syntax tree
Named entities like variables, functions, and classes stored in ___ tables used for compiler name resolution and type-checking
Compiler optimization that replaces a function call with its body to remove call overhead and enable further optimizations
Typed symbols produced during lexical analysis to simplify syntax analysis
